name: Sophie Tucker
description: Russian-born American singer, comedian, actress and radio personality
abstract: Sophie Tucker (born Sofia Kalish; January 13, 1886 – February 9, 1966) was a Jewish-American singer, comedian, actress, and radio personality. Known for her powerful delivery of comical and risqué songs, she was one of the most popular entertainers in the U.S.

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Mondello
about_quotes.evidence: So you grow up hearing all of these Sophie Tucker tunes and Fanny Brice tunes and tunes from the 20s and whatever.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nick Mondello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 19, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Tony Parenti and Anthony Sbarbaro
about_quotes.evidence: I was in there. It was a Black Cat. And there was a Tom Anderson... Overall. That was, uh, a place that we played there... during that time, that was the first time I had ever, ever seen Sophie Tucker... she was at one of those theaters... we went into our best little, uh, jazz tune was Tiger Rag...
about_quotes.doc_title: Tony Parenti and Anthony Sbarbaro Interview, 1959-06-29

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Greer
about_quotes.evidence: You heard of Sophie? ... So Sophie Tucker came over as soon as she come in, they introduced her, Miss Tucker's in the house. We played 'Some of These Days.' ... she come backstage and go Duke, said Duke, I want you to meet some friends of mine

about_quotes.speaker: John Altman
about_quotes.evidence: my childhood acquaintances were Nat King Cole and Judy Garland and the Andrews Sisters and Sophie Tucker, Jack Benny. So without knowing who they were, I was being imbued with this culture of music
about_quotes.doc_title: John Altman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 14, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Lizzie Miles
about_quotes.evidence: the first singer that I would go every week to see when she was here was Sophie Tucker... I'd hurry up and get my work through, so my mother would let me go up there to listen to her singing.

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: And, at that particular time, Frank Sinatra, as well as Sophie Tucker refused to work in New York in those big rooms unless they abolished that cabaret card business.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview
